title: Catch the model lying about numbers
problem: An AI assistant calculates profit margins from a CSV and says 'Beta Ltd has the highest margin at 62%'. The real number is 50%. Explain why this happens, and describe the validation step you would add to catch it before the answer reaches the user.
context:
CSV data:
business,revenue,cost
Alpha Corp,500000,480000
Beta Ltd,300000,150000
Gamma Inc,800000,820000
hints: LLMs predict plausible text, not calculated results.
The ground truth is in the CSV. Always compute the real value yourself.
A validation function compares the model's answer against the result you computed from raw data.
solution: Models hallucinate numbers because they generate plausible text, not verified calculations. The fix: compute the real answer yourself from the source data, then check whether the model's answer matches. If it doesn't, retry or override with the real value. Never trust a number the model gives you without checking it against the source.
